title: System Simplification

content.question: What can you cut in half this quarter without losing core value?
sop: Map your current process or offer step by step, no matter how small each step is.
Rate the Simplicity 1-10 of the current version and your Consistency 1-10 in actually running it (see calculation, page 4)
Cut any step that doesn't directly serve the core outcome.
Keep only the essential steps and re-test that the core value still holds.
Re-audit each quarter, complexity creeps back in even after you simplify.
questions: What can you cut in half this quarter without losing core value?
How many steps does your simplest offer actually take to deliver, and could half of them disappear?
What's still in your process because you built it once, not because it's still needed?
content.equation_example: Rate Simplicity 1-10 (how few steps or decisions the system requires) and Consistency 1-10 (how reliably you actually follow it). Simplicity 8 times Consistency 9 equals 72 out of 100. Higher means the system is both easy to run and actually gets run. Lower means either complexity or inconsistency is capping your execution.
content.closing_statement: A system too complex to follow won't get followed.

caption:
Principle Nº 143: System Simplification

Complexity kills execution.

Ask yourself: What can you cut in half this quarter without losing core value?
